title: Building the World, Brick by Brick: Oscar Nominee Florencia Martin on One Battle After Another
text: Oscar-nominated production designer Florencia Martin discusses her meticulous work on Paul Thomas Anderson's three-hour action thriller, which spans from redwoods to deserts across California and the American West. Martin built most sets from the ground up using real locations. For Sensei Sergio's apartment building in El Paso, she discovered Dennis's Perfumeria—a perfume shop near the border—and practically built Sensei's apartment upstairs using an existing trap door to a neighboring space. Rather than finding a derelict Spanish mission as originally envisioned, Martin selected an actual mission on California's mission trail. She aimed to create a space that felt hidden and believable where the group of women characters could credibly shelter. The Christmas Adventurers Club's underground bunker combined inspiration from Midwestern basement refurbishment companies with actual Sacramento and Stockton locations. Martin incorporated the perfect representation of the American West as the visual centerpiece of their meeting space. Martin constructed a 60-foot hand-hewn tunnel on LA North Studios stages, complete with a redwood stump emergence point—historically authentic to the lumber-logged region where residents converted tree stumps into outhouses and shelters. Explosions, car crashes, and the climactic three-car desert chase were executed practically.
